sourceMovie.title: Revenge
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Revenge (2017) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Coralie Fargeat's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Hunt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Revenge clicked for you, The Hunt (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Quick and the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Revenge clicked for you, The Quick and the Dead (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Revenge?
faq.answer: Try The Hunt, The Quick and the Dead, Grindhouse—each shares DNA with Revenge while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Revenge?
faq.answer: Revenge (2017) is best described as Action, Horror, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Revenge worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
